evidence.quote_or_summary: the monsoon and post-monsoon months — from late July to mid-October — are the best time to do this trek
The Chandrabhaga Glaciers Trek in July-August
The Chandrabhaga Glaciers Trek in September-October

values: After mid-October
Late season (post mid-October)

evidence.quote_or_summary: After mid-October, temperatures drop sharply and water sources begin to dry up. Once the snow starts to set in, the entire region gets cut off until the next summer. That’s why we don’t recommend attempting this trek too late in the season.

values: July-August: raw, rugged brown landscape with green patches near streams and snowmelt; snow in bits and pieces along trail (sometimes with icy base); streams full and fast from melting snow; mountains around Kya Tso and Ga Tso still snow-covered; lakes partly frozen with thin snow layer; wildflowers growing from rock cracks

September-October: classic cold desert with wide open brownish landscapes glowing golden, rust, or purple; loose boulders and pebbles on trail; easier stream crossings; clear blue skies and sharp views; jagged black mountain ridges; glaciers still hold ice; Kya Tso and Ga Tso fully thawed with turquoise blue colour; possible thin transparent ice sheet forming on lakes late in season

evidence.quote_or_summary: Late July to August is a dramatic time to do the Chandrabhaga Glaciers Trek. The landscape looks raw and rugged. Most of it is brown, with green patches near streams and snowmelt.
You’ll see snow in bits and pieces along the trail. Sometimes the base of this snow is ice — so tread carefully. Streams are full and fast, fed by melting snow.
The mountains around Kya Tso and Ga Tso are still covered in snow. Even the lakes are partly frozen. There’s a thin layer of snow on them, which slowly starts melting as the season progresses.
And just when you don’t expect it, you’ll spot wildflowers. They grow quietly from cracks in the rocks, adding colour to an otherwise barren land.

September and October bring a raw, striking beauty to the Chandrabhaga Glaciers Trek. Most of the snow has melted. What you see now is the classic cold desert — wide open, brownish landscapes that glow in different colours when the sun hits them. Sometimes golden, sometimes rust, even shades of purple.
With the snow gone, the trail feels tougher. You walk over loose boulders and pebbles, so make sure your legs are trained.

Streams are easier to cross now. The skies are clear and blue. The views are sharp. From the high points, you’ll see jagged black mountain ridges. And even though it’s late in the season, the glaciers still hold their ice, creating a magical contrast.

By then, the high-altitude lakes of Kya Tso and Ga Tso would have also fully thawed. They take on a stunning turquoise blue colour. If you’re trekking late in the season, you might even see a thin transparent sheet of ice forming on them.

values: Snow has thawed by late July except in bits/pieces along trail; mountains around Kya Tso and Ga Tso still snow-covered in July-August; lakes partly frozen with thin snow layer in July-August; by September-October most snow has melted except glacier ice; after mid-October snow sets in and region gets cut off

values: Lahaul lies in rain shadow of Pir Panjal range; no heavy rains like other parts of Himachal during monsoon/post-monsoon season

evidence.quote_or_summary: Since Lahaul lies in the rain shadow of the Pir Panjal range, you don’t face heavy rains like in other parts of Himachal during this season.

values: July-August: Daytime 8-10°C; nights may drop to 0°C at campsites
September-October: Daytime 4-6°C; nights drop several degrees below zero at campsites; wind chill makes it feel much colder; 3 warm layers required

Day time: Between 4-6 °C | Night time: Will drop several degrees below zero at campsites
No. of warm layers required: 3 warm layers
The wind chill can make it feel much colder than it actually is.

The skies are clear and blue. The views are sharp. From the high points, you’ll see jagged black mountain ridges.
This is the best time for big mountain views.
